name: pdf-analyze
description: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms. When Claude needs to fill in a PDF form or programmatically process, generate, or analyze PDF documents at scale.
capabilities: Extract text from PDF documents with layout preservation
Extract tables from PDFs and convert to structured formats like Excel
Fill fillable PDF forms with field validation
Add text annotations to non-fillable PDF forms
Convert PDF pages to images for visual analysis
author: 21pounder
securitySummary: This is a legitimate PDF processing toolkit. The static analyzer flagged 173 potential issues, but ALL are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are Markdown code formatting delimiters, not actual command execution. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are test fixture data with hash-like strings, not crypto operations. 'C2 keywords' refers to 'encrypt' in PDF password documentation. No network exfiltration, credential theft, or obfuscation patterns exist. The skill's behavior fully aligns with its stated purpose.
valueStatement: PDF documents often contain important data that is difficult to access programmatically. This skill provides Claude with comprehensive tools to extract text and tables, fill forms, merge documents, and convert PDFs to images for analysis.
userTitle: Process PDF documents for extraction and form filling
limitations: Requires poppler-utils installed for command-line tools
OCR requires tesseract installation for scanned documents
Cannot edit existing PDF content beyond form fields and annotations
Complex PDF forms may require manual verification of output

useCases.title: Extract tables from reports
useCases.description: Pull structured data from financial reports, research papers, and statistical documents into CSV or Excel format.

useCases.title: Automate form completion
useCases.description: Fill out PDF forms programmatically with validated data for applications, surveys, and official documents.

useCases.title: Build PDF processing workflows
useCases.description: Create document processing pipelines that merge, split, and transform PDFs for applications and services.

promptTemplates.title: Extract PDF text
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract all text from document.pdf using pdfplumber and save it to extracted_text.txt

promptTemplates.title: List form fields
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if application_form.pdf has fillable form fields, and if so, list all field names and types

promptTemplates.title: Extract tables
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract all tables from quarterly_report.pdf and save them to an Excel file with one sheet per table

promptTemplates.title: Fill PDF form
promptTemplates.prompt: Fill in the following fields in application_form.pdf using data from field_values.json and save to completed_form.pdf

bestPractices: Validate form field values before submission to catch errors early
Convert PDF to images first when working with non-fillable forms to visually verify annotation placement
Use the bounding box validation script to ensure annotations do not overlap or obscure existing content
antiPatterns: Skipping the form field validation step before filling PDFs
Not converting non-fillable PDFs to images for visual analysis first
Using hardcoded file paths instead of parameters for reusability

faq.question: Which Python libraries does this skill use?
faq.answer: Primary libraries are pypdf for basic operations, pdfplumber for text and table extraction, and reportlab for creating new PDFs.

faq.question: What are the system requirements?
faq.answer: Requires Python 3.8+ with pip install of pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab, pdf2image, and PIL. Poppler must be installed for PDF to image conversion.

faq.question: How do I fill a scanned PDF that is not fillable?
faq.answer: Use the non-fillable form workflow: convert PDF to images, manually determine text entry locations, create fields.json with bounding boxes, then use fill_pdf_form_with_annotations.py.

faq.question: Is my data safe when processing PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. All processing is local using Python libraries. No data is sent to external servers. Files are only read from and written to paths you specify.

faq.question: Why does my filled PDF show annotations in the wrong position?
faq.answer: This usually indicates incorrect coordinate transformation. PDF coordinates start from bottom-left while image coordinates start from top-left. Verify your bounding box conversion logic.

faq.question: How is this different from using pdf-lib in JavaScript?
faq.answer: The Python tools provide more mature text extraction and table parsing. pdf-lib is better suited for browser environments or Node.js projects that need to create or modify PDFs client-side.
